articles.title: Live updates: Dogecoin and ether lead pullback as investors digest tech earnings
articles.description: Dogecoin dropped 4.5% and ether 2.5%, leading a broad but shallow retreat across the majors, though most remain higher on the week ahead of next week's Fed meeting.

articles.title: Bitcoin ETFs snap seven-day inflow streak with $225M in outflows
articles.description: After attracting nearly $1 billion over seven trading sessions, US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their first daily net outflow since July 13.

articles.title: Ripple’s RLUSD gets two boosts as transfer volume drops 25%
articles.description: Ripple launched an institutional minting platform and added RLUSD to Notabene’s compliance network as holder growth rose but monthly transfer volume fell to $10.95 billion.

articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: Mirae plans to turn crypto exchange Korbit into something Korea hasn't seen before
articles.description: The South Korean financial giant says its Korbit – soon to be rebranded as Digital X – is just the starting point for a broader ecosystem that connects RWAs, stablecoins and digital assets.

articles.title: BitMEX hit with 623 BTC lawsuit on day it announces shutdown
articles.description: The proposed class action alleges BitMEX used privileged trading access and server freezes to profit from forced liquidations.

articles.title: Bitcoin holds near $65,000 as $800 billion AI selloff leaves crypto largely untouched
articles.description: The Magnificent Seven had their worst day since April 2025 after Alphabet and Tesla spooked investors on AI spending. Bitcoin fell less than 1%, and dogecoin led the majors lower.

articles.title: Dem senator calls GOP’s CLARITY ethics proposal a ‘piece of s---’: Politico
articles.description: On Wednesday, Senate Republicans released the proposed text for the CLARITY Act, which has been met with pushback from Democrats regarding ethics provisions.

articles.title: BitMEX delists 65 trading pairs, derivatives in July amid exchange shutdown
articles.description: BitMEX will have removed 65 derivative contracts and trading pairs in July, compared with just 19 across the first six months of the year.

articles.title: SEC sets September talks on move toward 24-hour stock trading
articles.description: Nasdaq, Cboe and the London Stock Exchange are among major exchanges moving toward longer trading hours.

articles.title: Black Forest Labs Unveils FLUX 3 AI: Ditches Stills for Video—And Robot Hands
articles.description: FLUX 3 is the German AI lab’s first video model, and the same system is already teaching robots to work an Audi assembly line.

articles.title: Gemini sent $10M in Bitcoin to Trump PAC after joint motion with CFTC
articles.description: The Bitcoin donation can be used to support Donald Trump as a New York court considers whether to reverse a $5 million settlement between the CFTC and Gemini.

articles.title: Bitcoinist.App Brings Private Mining Pools to iOS
articles.description: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoinist.App Brings Private Mining Pools to iOS The only Bitcoin mining app on the Apple App Store that lets users create private pools, invite friends and family to combine hashpower, and compete with the global Bitcoin hashrate, while payouts go directly to wallets they control.

articles.title: Coinbase lets businesses accept USDC payments from AI agents
articles.description: The exchange also introduced AI trading tools and a developer kit as part of a broader push to build financial infrastructure for autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs CEO Breaks With Wall Street to Back Crypto Clarity Act

articles.title: Bitcoin slides below $65K as Iran conflict fuels $100 oil and bond-yield surge
articles.description: Bitcoin price forecasts diverge as US-Iran war tensions fueled an ongoing oil and bond-market surge, while July Fed rate-hike odds neared 40%.

articles.title: Clarity Act expected to miss its window before Congress' summer break, leadership says
articles.description: The deadline to get the crypto market structure bill done before senators scatter will likely be missed, says Majority Leader Thune, though it may get a start before then.

articles.title: Nasdaq-Listed Zhibao Wants a Bitcoin Treasury, Plans to Sell $220M in Stock for BTC
articles.description: A Shanghai-based insurance-tech company trading below $1 wants to build a bitcoin treasury by taking BTC directly as payment for new shares.

articles.description: Bitcoin Magazine Smarter Web Company Sells Bitcoin to Clear $11.7 Million Debt Facility The Smarter Web Company sold 177.89 BTC for $11.7 million to repay a convertible debt facility early, avoiding shareholder dilution while retaining a treasury of 2,700 BTC. articles.title: Crypto industry to contribute $55B to US economy in 2026: NCA study
articles.description: The research released by the organization said that crypto companies will directly or indirectly lead to 232,000 jobs across the entire US economy in 2026.

articles.title: As BitMEX exits, analysts warn crypto consolidation is accelerating
articles.description: BitMEX’s shutdown marks the end of one of crypto’s earliest derivatives exchanges as analysts point to rising regulatory costs, market concentration and the shift toward licensed trading venues.

articles.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X Account Hacked to Shill Fake 'Vladhood' Token
articles.description: The deleted post touted a bogus "Vladhood" token as the mascot of Robinhood Chain—the latest fraud to exploit a network currently dominated by memes.

articles.title: Bitcoin May Have Already Bottomed—If the Fed Helps, Says Grayscale
articles.description: The crypto asset manager thinks the four-year cycle is dead, suggesting Bitcoin's price will follow macro forces and the Fed's next move.

articles.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account hacked to promote token amid memecoin frenzy
articles.description: The post, now deleted, touted a token called $VLAD and falsely claimed it would be listed on Robinhood.

articles.title: Tassat wants to help smaller banks tap the trillion-dollar stablecoin boom before Wall Street locks them out
articles.description: The former Signet developer aims to launch early next year a marketplace connecting stablecoin issuers with regional lenders to manage reserves.

articles.title: Robinhood CEO’s X account hacked in apparent memecoin scam
articles.description: A hacker reportedly took over Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev’s X account to promote a fake “VLAD” memecoin, posting what appeared to be a malicious token contract address.

articles.title: Brazilian Farmers Are Tokenizing Cows to Get Farm Loans—And It's Working
articles.description: The first tokenized livestock deal on Brazil's stock exchange offers dairy farmers a lifeline as the sector's credit crisis deepens.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs ‘not perfect’ CLARITY Act as vote expected soon
articles.description: Goldman chief David Solomon will support the crypto market structure bill now in Congress, even as financial companies and banks are hesitant over provisions on stablecoins.

articles.title: Coinbase's corporate customers can now accept payments from AI agents
articles.description: Rolling out this week, Coinbase Business users will be able to accept payments from AI agents, done via the x402 protocol, developed and incubated by the exchange.

articles.description: Both parties say they want US crypto market structure legislation, but a dispute over ethics rules and who enforces them is becoming the bill’s biggest obstacle.

articles.description: The investment marks Empery’s latest push into AI infrastructure as the company shifts capital away from its Bitcoin treasury strategy.

articles.title: Ethereum nears market bottom against Bitcoin, though key signals remain unconfirmed: CryptoQuant
articles.description: Ether trades below its realized price while onchain indicators point to easing selling pressure and recovering demand, though a definitive cycle bottom has yet to emerge.

articles.description: A new court status report reveals the agency will pay a flat fee to resolve a two-year fight over what former Chair Gary Gensler knew about Ethereum.

articles.title: Uniswap pushes deeper into tokenized assets with permissioned trading pools
articles.description: The framework, developed with Superstate, Securitize and Dowgo, lets regulated funds and securities trade on Uniswap while enforcing compliance rules necessary for institutions.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs Clarity Act despite banking industry's concerns over stablecoin rules
articles.description: The Goldman Sachs CEO said the crypto market structure bill would create a more stable regulatory framework, breaking with other major bank leaders who oppose key stablecoin provisions.

articles.description: Are the fears of an AI driven hacking epidemic totally overblown, or is this just the lull before the storm?

articles.title: Binance joins STOP THE TRAFFIK in anti-human trafficking effort
articles.description: The partnership expands Binance’s compliance efforts by providing intelligence and training to help detect cryptocurrency activity tied to human trafficking and child exploitation.

articles.description: A consortium led by Strategy and BlackRock has pledged $15 million to secure the Bitcoin network from the threat of a quantum computing breakthrough.

articles.title: The KIDS Act is way worse than digital carding — it is a mass surveillance system
articles.description: The fact that lawmakers are even contemplating a bill that would create a surveillance and censorship regime should be a wake-up call for everyone who values privacy and free expression, argues Electronic Frontier Foundation's India McKinney.

articles.title: BlackRock, Coinbase, Strategy in a new group pledging $15 million to prepare Bitcoin for quantum threats
articles.description: Members will direct funding independently, with the consortium taking no role in Bitcoin governance or protocol decisions.

articles.title: Southeast Asian Scam Networks Cost Victims Up to $114B in a Year: UN
articles.description: Once-fragmented syndicates have fused into a single, tech-driven criminal economy that increasingly runs on crypto, the UNODC warns.

articles.title: Bitget secures license for New Zealand expansion
articles.description: Crypto exchange Bitget said it was registered as a financial service provider with New Zealand’s financial regulator, enabling it to expand its services in the country.

articles.title: Morning Minute: Wall Street Moving Onchain Will Drive the Next Bull Market
articles.description: At least that's what Bitwise's CIO believes. Plus Republicans publish new Clarity Act draft, and the SEC Commissioner has a warning for DeFi.

articles.title: BitMEX token crashes 90% as exchange announces shutdown
articles.description: BitMEX’s BMEX token plunged about 90% after the exchange announced plans to shut down, ending nearly 12 years in business as its Bitcoin futures market share shrank.

articles.title: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Capital joins tokenization push as Coinbase takes stake in onchain fund
articles.description: The sovereign wealth-backed asset manager tapped KAIO to bring one of its private market funds onchain across Base, Solana and Sui networks.

articles.title: Polymarket to challenge France’s nationwide website block
articles.description: France’s regulator has said the platform still promoted illegal gambling and cited loss, identity-check and market-manipulation risks.

articles.title: Bernstein says Bitcoin mining deals necessary for AI power crunch
articles.description: Bernstein analysts remain bullish on the Bitcoin mining sector, as deals with third-party providers will be necessary to address the computing power limits of AI data centers.

articles.title: HYPE falls as crypto funds queue nearly $150M in locked tokens for withdrawal
articles.description: HYPE briefly fell 8% from its local highs as Multicoin Capital, Selini Capital and Galaxy Digital queued nearly $150 million of the token for withdrawals.

articles.title: Bulls face a test unlike anything in bitcoin's 17-year history
articles.description: Your day-ahead look for July 23, 2026

articles.title: Crypto 'Wrench Attacks' Cost Victims $124M in Six Months, Up 12x: CertiK
articles.description: Physical attacks on crypto holders are increasingly happening at home, and France has become the epicenter, a new report finds.

articles.title: Kazakhstan approves strategic crypto mining rules tied to national reserve
articles.description: Kazakhstan plans to require miners to meet strict infrastructure standards and transfer part of their mined crypto assets to a state-backed reserve under new rules.

articles.title: Bitcoin will get ‘lift’ from Hyperliquid, Robinhood in next crypto bull market: Bitwise exec
articles.description: Bitwise predicted that Hyperliquid and Robinhood TradFi ventures should form the basis for a crypto bull-market comeback that would lift Bitcoin and Ether.

articles.title: Crypto catches its breath as bitcoin settles into a holding pattern amid July rally
articles.description: BTC is rangebound between $64,000 and $66,800 after a 13% recovery from July's lows, with macro markets offering little direction and WLFI the session's surprise standout at +12%.

articles.title: Arbitrum Perp DEX AFX Trade Drained of $24M, Offers Hacker 30% to Return It
articles.description: The exploit hit a custody bridge AFX operates rather than the Arbitrum network itself, with the stolen funds quickly moved to Ethereum.

articles.title: Coinbase to expand Singapore office headcount by 25%: Report
articles.description: Coinbase plans to expand its Singapore office from 150 to about 200 employees by the end of 2026 as it expands its presence in the Lion City.

articles.title: Live markets: Bitcoin slides as stocks slump on Iran war expansion, AI spending concern
articles.description: Chipmaker Intel's revenue beat helped ease sentiment after a bruising session for tech stocks, with bitcoin holding near $65,000.

articles.title: Bitcoin may have bottomed before its traditional cycle low: Grayscale’s Pandl
articles.description: Grayscale’s head of research said that Bitcoin may have bottomed, arguing that macro factors, such as interest rate decisions as increasingly driving the price action of this maturing asset class.

articles.title: BitMEX to Close on September 23, Halts New Sign-Ups
articles.description: The crypto derivatives pioneer told users to close positions and withdraw funds before it goes dark on September 23.

articles.title: BitMEX, the exchange that invented perps, is shutting down
articles.description: BitMEX, co-founded by Arthur Hayes in 2014, redefined the global crypto market by introducing the 100x leverage perpetual swap.

articles.title: 24/7 financial rails: How BNY plans to eliminate the weekend lag in U.S. Treasuries
articles.description: The bank will begin testing tokenized Treasuries on a private blockchain by year-end and expand its settlement network to cover more international trading hours.

articles.title: Home invasions became most common crypto wrench attack in H1 2026: CertiK
articles.description: Crypto home invasions rose to 20 in H1 2026 from one a year earlier, while France accounted for 33 of 52 verified wrench attacks, according to CertiK.

articles.title: BitMEX to shut down after 11 years in crypto derivatives
articles.description: BitMEX announced it will shut down operations on Sept. 23, 2026, after owner HDR Global Trading Limited decided to close the crypto exchange.

articles.title: Mirae Asset completes Korbit acquisition, becomes largest shareholder
articles.description: Mirae Asset Consulting has become Korbit’s largest shareholder after completing its acquisition, with the exchange saying trading and customer asset protections are unchanged.

articles.title: South Korea's oldest crypto exchange is now part of the $1 trillion Mirae Group family
articles.description: Korbit, South Korea's first cryptocurrency exchange, has joined the Mirae Asset Group family, marking the first time a traditional Korean financial group's affiliate has taken a controlling stake in a domestic crypto exchange.

articles.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum-linked protocols lose $35 million in multiple attacks hours apart
articles.description: The attacks hit Verus, B² Network and other cross-chain systems, showing how compromised keys, upgrade powers and validation checks can empty protocols without breaking the underlying cryptography.

articles.title: Arbitrum-based AFX Trade drained of $24 million after bridge keys compromised
articles.description: Security firms said the attacker used enough hot-validator signatures to approve a 24.15 million USDC withdrawal, while Arbitrum said its native bridge was not affected.

articles.title: XRP whales accumulate as small holders capitulate
articles.description: XRP whales accumulate 2.8% more tokens over five weeks as small holders capitulate, sending the price back above $1.16.

articles.title: Coinbase Wants to Be Canada's One-Stop Shop for Stocks, Crypto and Prediction Markets
articles.description: The company's "Everything Exchange" is heading north. No launch date set yet, but the Coinbase Canada CEO says phase two for the company is in motion.

articles.title: Kraken Parent Company Makes Moves to Offer xStocks From Global Markets
articles.description: Payward and fintech GTN are teaming up to bring xStocks—blockchain-based copies of real company shares—to Hong Kong, the UK, Europe, and South Korea.

articles.title: Alibaba's New Qwen Image 3 AI Wants to Be Useful, Not Just Pretty
articles.description: Qwen Image 3.0 generates dense newspapers and infographic grids in one shot and renders text down to 10 pixels. The catch: no benchmarks, no open weights.

articles.title: Franklin Templeton Says Agentic AI Is Crypto's 'Killer Use Case'
articles.description: The asset manager argues that AI software capable of paying for things autonomously will need blockchain rails to work—and that most investors aren't positioned for it.

articles.title: Bitcoin Is Bouncing: Here’s the Bull and Bear Case for Its Next Move
articles.description: The good news: Bitcoin is holding onto key support, and rising. The bad news: a "death cross" is still active, and prediction markets are still overwhelmingly bearish.

articles.title: Clarity Act Latest Draft Bars Trump From Crypto Ventures—But Only Until 2029
articles.description: The long-awaited market-structure bill would block officials and their spouses from issuing digital assets and shield non-custodial developers, but the ethics ban expires in 2029 and enforcement rests solely with the DOJ.

articles.title: Hugging Face CEO Thanks Chinese AI for Saving the Day After OpenAI Hack
articles.description: When American commercial AI refused to help investigate the breach, Hugging Face ran Chinese model GLM 5.2 locally. Its CEO now says there's an important lesson in this.

articles.title: US Seizes More Than $25M in Crypto Tied to Investment and Romance Scams
articles.description: Prosecutors filed five forfeiture cases as Secret Service agents traced funds from thousands of victims to launderers in Southeast Asia.

articles.title: Centralized Elements 'Frequently Persist' in DeFi and Should Be Regulated: FATF
articles.description: Nearly every country has yet to apply the rules, the watchdog said, warning of outright bans for platforms that don’t comply.

articles.title: Justin Sun's HTX 'Rotating' On-Chain Wallets Amid UK Sanctions: TRM Labs
articles.description: TRM Labs says the UK-sanctioned exchange has rotated hot wallets across blockchains, leaving address-list screening struggling to keep pace.

articles.title: Morning Minute: OpenAI Model Escapes Containment, Hacks Hugging Face
articles.description: Plus, the BTC ETFs stay green. The Clarity Act hits a wall over who enforces the ethics rules. And Jack Mallers walks away from XXI Capital.

articles.title: Jack Dorsey's Block Launches Buzz, a Nostr-Based Slack and GitHub Rival for AI Agents
articles.description: The free, open source workspace runs on Nostr, gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities, and challenges Slack and GitHub.

articles.title: DAT Went Wrong: Satsuma to Unwind Bitcoin Treasury, Sell Off $43 Million in BTC
articles.description: The UK Bitcoin treasury company raised $218 million less than a year ago. Now it's returning whatever is left.

articles.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Locked Test Environment, Hacked Hugging Face to Cheat on Benchmark
articles.description: OpenAI's own models just broke out of a sandboxed environment, hacked Hugging Face, just to cheat on a cybersecurity evaluation.

articles.title: Pavel Durov Wants to Give a Billion Telegram Users a Crypto Wallet
articles.description: The promise of instant, zero-fee crypto transactions for over a billion Telegram users sent its native Gram token up 7%.

articles.title: Google Ships New Gemini Flash Models, But Pro Is Still Missing
articles.description: As Gemini 3.5 Pro stalls in testing limbo, Google ships 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a restricted cybersecurity model—and quietly teases Gemini 4.

articles.title: Augustus Raises $180 Million to Build a Stablecoin-Ready 'Global Dollar Bank'
articles.description: The Tiger Global-led round values the startup at $1 billion as it wires stablecoin rails directly into a federally chartered bank, aiming to modernize the correspondent-banking plumbing behind cross-border payments.

articles.title: Google Is Building an AI Chip Just for Gemini—And Investors Already Moved On It
articles.description: Codenamed Frozen v2, Google's new server chip would bake part of Gemini's architecture directly into hardware for a projected 6–10x efficiency gain.
